# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for CI/CD systems, restricted to an AWS Organization.

Parameters:
  AWSOrganizationID:
    Type: String
    Description: The ID of your AWS Organization (e.g., 'o-xxxxxxxxxx'). This is used to restrict cross-account access to only accounts within your organization.

Resources:
  CICDCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: CICDCrossAccountRole
      Description: IAM role allowing cross-account assumption by CI/CD systems within the specified AWS Organization.
      MaxSessionDuration: 3600 # Default is 1 hour, can be increased up to 12 hours (43200 seconds)
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              # By specifying "AWS: '*'", we allow any principal to attempt assumption.
              # This is then strictly scoped by the Condition block below
              # to only allow principals from accounts within the specified AWS Organization.
              AWS: "*"
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
              - sts:TagSession # Optional: Allows passing session tags during assumption
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                # This condition ensures that only principals from accounts belonging
                # to the specified AWS Organization ID can assume this role.
                # This provides a flexible "wildcard" mechanism for all accounts
                # within your organization, including new ones added in the future.
                "aws:PrincipalOrgID": !Ref AWSOrganizationID
